review_english: Well, let's start with what I want to make clear, I consider this game to be the benchmark among shooters. I am amazed how the developers managed to combine the two gameplays of a fast pilot who runs on walls and takes the heads of enemies in flight, and a large titan in some cases slow or fast. The big plus is that you will find your style of play and you will not get bored either when choosing a weapon or when choosing your favorite metal titan friend, at least that was the case for me. The plus point is that this game will most likely exist until the servers are shut down, so if you wanted to play it but haven't dared yet, I advise you to try the pluses of the game is the speed of the gameplay, the game always does not let you get bored and rewards you for your skill or competent positioning, which is important in a pilot game, but even more important in a titan game. Plus, the game has story mode in my opinion, the only thing that spoils it is the villains, who look cartoonish at some points, but personally, it didn't stop me from going through the story mode at least 5 times on all difficulty levels. What can definitely be attributed to the negatives is that it is difficult for a newcomer to get into this game, because there is a fairly formed community and this leads to a lot of very skilled people who sometimes do not let you play. Unfortunately, there is another small problem with the balance due to the lack of game support, all these elements will remain. For example, too powerful a weapon if your opponent, let's say, has a car and you didn't catch him by surprise, you usually lose this 1v1 fight, although in multiplayer you can be killed by so many things that I personally got used to it on the other hand, pilots have their own tactical skills that can help you turn the tide of the battle or save your life, for example, becoming invisible or releasing your hologram to fool the enemy so if you like fast paced gameplay and mechs I recommend this game plus the game has a PVE mode where you can test your titans and run around on your two as a pilot and basically I personally enjoy fending off waves of enemies sitting in my titan so maybe this mode doesn't help much in terms of learning the mechs but it's definitely a breath of fresh air if you're tired of online matches sometimes drop in with 3 by random players or friends and destroy waves of creeps what is necessary. Of course, I did not delve into all the details and details, but I tried to convey my emotions and impressions. Thank you for your attention. See you at the Frontier, pilot

review:
几年前，有一位同学语文课前的分享，介绍了《泰坦陨落2》这款游戏，他用无比浪漫的语言、无比精彩的画面向我们描绘他所热爱的机甲战场。但如果只是这样，一个十分钟的分享不会让我记住这么多年。在演讲的最后，他，一个高中生，面对同学和老师们，大谈自己对游戏、梦想、人生的看法，激情澎湃，意气风发。在最高潮处，他说道：“富贵非吾愿，帝乡不可期”。台下的同学都听懵了，老师也懵了——一个即将高考的学生竟说出如此非主流的观点，我知道，他不是说给我们听的，他在说给自己听。
  多年以后，和他再谈起这件事时，再谈起年少的梦，他也渐渐模糊起来。
  但你，我的朋友，你的说语实实在在地激励了一个人，这条路或许疯狂，或许并不可行，但是他会不回头地走下去。
  致我们浪漫无悔的青春！

review_english: A few years ago, a classmate shared before the Chinese class and introduced the game Titanfall 2. He used extremely romantic language and extremely wonderful pictures to describe his beloved mecha battlefield to us. But if it were just like this, a ten-minute sharing would not let me remember it for so many years. At the end of the speech, he, a high school student, talked about his views on games, dreams, and life in front of his classmates and teachers. He was passionate and high-spirited. At the climax, he said, "Wealth is not my wish, and the imperial land is not expected." The students in the audience were stunned, and the teacher was also stunned. A student who was about to take the college entrance examination actually said such an unconventional view. I knew he was not telling us, he was telling himself. Many years later, when I talked about this matter with him again, he gradually became blurry when talking about his youthful dreams. But you, my friend, your words truly inspired a person. This road may be crazy or not feasible, but he will go on without looking back, giving us a romantic and regretless youth.

review:
If you want one of the most fun FPS campaigns ever made, Titanfall 2 on is absolutely worth playing. The campaign puts you in the role of Jack Cooper, a rifleman who unexpectedly becomes linked with a giant Titan named BT, and the relationship between the two honestly carries the whole game. The gunplay feels insanely smooth, with wall running, double jumps, sliding, and fast movement that makes almost every fight feel cinematic. Then you combine that with giant mech battles where you pilot BT, and the game constantly switches between fast parkour combat and heavy Titan action in a way that never gets boring.
The graphics and level design still hold up really well too. The environments are detailed, colorful, and varied, ranging from massive industrial facilities to beautiful futuristic landscapes. Some missions are genuinely unforgettable, especially the time manipulation level that people still talk about years later because of how creative it is. The action feels huge without becoming messy, and the game always keeps the pacing exciting.

The only real downside is that the campaign is pretty short. You can finish it in around 6–8 hours, which might disappoint people looking for a super long story. But honestly, the campaign is so consistently good that it never feels dragged out or filled with boring sections. Instead of wasting your time with filler, it delivers one amazing mission after another and leaves a strong impression from start to finish.

Overall, Titanfall 2’s campaign is one of those games where almost everyone finishes it wishing there was more. Even though it’s short, the movement, combat, visuals, and story make it one of the best single player FPS experiences you can get .
